section.title: AI and work
section.intro: How AI changes the experience of professional work – productivity, expertise, identity, and who benefits from the gains.

resources.title: AI Doesn't Reduce Work – It Intensifies It
resources.author: Aruna Ranganathan & Xingqi Maggie Ye
resources.description: Eight-month study of AI tool adoption at a tech company. Found work intensified rather than decreased – organizations got more output, not less work.

resources.title: Why Gen AI Feels So Threatening to Workers
resources.description: Identifies three threats beyond job loss: competence (AI approximates expert output), autonomy (decisions shift to systems workers don't control), and belonging (team identity shifts when core tasks change).

resources.title: How Do Workers Develop Good Judgment in the AI Era?
resources.description: Senior professionals gain from AI because they can evaluate its output. Junior staff can't yet – they need to build judgment through doing the work AI now handles.

resources.title: Management as AI Superpower
resources.author: Ethan Mollick
resources.description: Argues that working with AI agents requires management skills, not prompt engineering. Delegating, reviewing, providing context, and catching errors are the same skills that make a good manager.

resources.title: AI Adoption Accelerating, Confidence Collapsing
resources.description: Survey data showing trust in company AI programs fell 31% in three months while adoption accelerated.

resources.title: The Anthropic Economic Index
resources.description: Analysis of Claude usage patterns. Found a net deskilling pattern – AI covers higher-education tasks first, not manual labor.

resources.title: The AI Precariat: Davos Experts Warn of a New Class of Worker
resources.description: Identifies a class of workers whose skills are perpetually one model release away from devaluation. Focuses on knowledge workers, not manual labor.

resources.title: Algorithmic Anxiety: A Systematic Review
resources.description: Systematic review of the psychological effects of working alongside AI decision systems – anxiety about algorithmic evaluation and uncertainty about when to override AI recommendations.

resources.title: Navigating the Jagged Technological Frontier
resources.author: Dell'Acqua, Mollick, et al.
resources.description: 758 BCG consultants using AI finished 12% more tasks, 25% faster, at 40% higher quality. But AI's capability boundary is "jagged" – it excels at some tasks and fails silently at others.

resources.title: Intuition and Taste in the Age of AI
resources.source: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
resources.description: Argues that when evidence collection becomes automated, what remains is judgment – the ability to sense patterns before you can name them. Discernment matters more, not less, as AI removes friction between measurement and action.

resources.title: AI isn't useless. But is it worth it?
resources.author: Molly White
resources.description: AI is useful for some things (simple coding, proofreading). Weighs the genuine utility against environmental cost, labor displacement, and the hype cycle.

resources.title: AI Changed Work Forever in 2025
resources.description: Year-in-review of how AI reshaped professional work in 2025 through quiet integration into daily workflows, changing expectations faster than policies could keep up.
